event: implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
found lying at home with her family
transported to our hospital by ambulance
no clear neurological deficits were observed
non-contrast head CT revealed hemorrhage in the third and fourth ventricles
hemorrhage in bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
hemorrhage was confined within the ventricles
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
cerebral angiograph revealed an aneurysm (3.8 mm) at a distal site of the mLSA from the proximal A1 segment of the left ACA
guiding sheath was navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
Marathon catheter was advanced to the left A1
a 33% NBCA-Lipiodol mixture was slowly injected
angiography after NBCA injection demonstrated disappearance of the aneurysm
no signs of hemorrhagic complications or cerebral infarction on postoperative head CT
neither impaired consciousness nor paralysis developed
lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
a decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
after 6-month rehabilitation, she was discharged to home
